title: Best AI Agent Platforms (2026) | Salesforce UK
title_zh: 最佳AI代理平台（2026年）| Salesforce英国
full_text:
Share the story
Try Agentforce
Are you feeling the pressure to do more with less? You're not alone. From automotive to aviation, companies in every industry are looking for innovative ways to streamline operations and improve customer experiences. AI agent platforms offer a promising solution, enabling businesses to develop, deploy, and manage autonomous AI agents to execute tasks and achieve specific goals with minimal human intervention.
AI agent platforms help organisations save time and money, boost productivity, and enhance decision-making across key business functions. This guide will explore the essential components, benefits, real-world use cases, and future trends of this transformative technology.
At its core, an AI agent is a type of artificial intelligence system that can understand and respond to customer inquiries without human intervention. They are created through an agent builder , such as Agentforce and use machine learning and natural language processing (NLP) to handle a variety of tasks. Agents thrive on high-quality data, pulling in and synthesising information from disparate sources to inform their decision-making and actions.
AI agent platforms are characterised by these essential capabilities:
Autonomy: Agents operate independently, making decisions and taking action without continuous human intervention.
Learning: Agents continuously improve their performance by adapting and refining strategies based on experience, leading to enhanced outcomes over time.
Adaptability: Agents readily adjust to evolving environments and diverse tasks, maintaining effectiveness even when faced with novel challenges.
Goal-oriented: Agents are designed to achieve defined objectives, whether it's optimising workflows, enhancing customer experiences, or managing complex operations.
Security: Robust measures are implemented to ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of agents, their data, and their operations.
Interaction & communication: Agents can effectively interact and communicate with other agents, diverse systems, and potentially humans through various methods, enabling seamless collaboration and information exchange.
An AI agent platform is more than just a single piece of software; it's a complete AI, data, and security ecosystem that provides all the necessary tools and infrastructure for developing and managing AI agents. The essential components include:
Component
Function
Agent builder
Facilitates the creation of AI agents
Reasoning engine
Analyses data, identifies patterns, and makes decisions
Action
Executes tasks and interacts with users or other systems
Learning
Improves performance over time through feedback and new data
Orchestration
Coordinates multiple agents to work together on complex workflows
Governance
Applies security, compliance, and operational controls
Agent Builder offers an accessible, AI-driven, low-code tool for building and customising AI agents to meet any business need. Users can describe an agent's purpose using natural language, leverage pre-built topics to define the agent's scope of work, and seamlessly integrate with knowledge bases and existing business logic.
Intuitive builder environment: Agent Builder offers a user-friendly, AI-powered, low-code interface for visually designing and configuring your AI agents. This “digital workshop” provides a streamlined experience for users of all technical levels.
Low-code focus with pro-code extensibility: Agent Builder emphasises a low-code approach, allowing you to build agents through configuration and natural language descriptions. However, it also provides the flexibility to extend agent capabilities using pro-code tools like Apex Classes and integrations with other Salesforce development resources.
AI-Powered Assistance: Agent Builder can assist in suggesting topics and actions and even generate initial agent configurations based on your descriptions and Salesforce metadata.
Seamless Salesforce Integration: Agent Builder is deeply integrated with the Salesforce ecosystem, allowing you to easily connect agents to your existing Salesforce data, business logic (Flows, Apex), APIs (MuleSoft), and pre-built Prompt Templates.
Built-in Testing and Monitoring: Agent Builder includes tools like Plan Tracer, enabling you to preview and test how your agents will respond to queries, ensuring optimal performance and facilitating iterative development.
Find out more
AI knowledge bases are integral components of modern data and AI platforms, serving as comprehensive repositories that store, organise, and manage a wide array of data and insights. These repositories are designed to support AI-driven decision-making and automation, ensuring that AI agents and systems have access to the most relevant and up-to-date information.
Key features include:
Real-time insights : AI Knowledge Bases provide real-time access to data, enabling AI agents to make immediate and informed decisions. This is crucial for dynamic environments where data is constantly changing.
Data integration : These knowledge bases seamlessly integrate with various data sources, including structured and unstructured data, ensuring a holistic view of the information. This integration supports comprehensive data analysis and more accurate AI-driven insights.
Data governance : Robust data governance features ensure that the information in the knowledge base is secure, compliant, and of high quality. This is essential for maintaining trust and reliability in AI-driven processes.
AI agents can seamlessly access and utilise the knowledge stored in these bases to perform tasks. By providing agents with a robust and dynamic knowledge base, organisations can ensure that their AI systems make data-driven decisions, leading to fewer errors and biases and ultimately improving outcomes.
Implementing an AI agent platform can bring a wealth of benefits to your organisation, fundamentally transforming the way you operate, interact with customers, and ultimately achieve business objectives. This software moves beyond simple automation, offering intelligent assistance and proactive capabilities that can drive significant improvements across various facets of the enterprise.
Increased efficiency and productivity: By automating repetitive and time-consuming tasks, AI agents free up your employees to focus on more strategic and creative work. This leads to increased efficiency and productivity across the board. Automating tasks, saving time and money, and boosting productivity are key advantages.
Reduced costs: AI agent platforms can improve resource utilisation by optimising processes and reducing waste.
Improved customer experience: AI agents are at your service around the clock, providing immediate responses to customer queries, which can significantly boost satisfaction and loyalty. Moreover, they can customise interactions to cater to each customer's distinct needs and preferences. The convenience of 24/7 customer support is invaluable.
Enhanced decision-making: AI agent software solutions have the ability to analyse large volumes of data, offering data-driven insights and recommendations that can lead to more informed decisions.
Scalability and flexibility: AI agent platforms can easily handle increasing workloads, ensuring that your automation efforts can scale as your business grows. They are also adaptable to changing business needs, allowing you to quickly adjust your automation strategies to meet new challenges.
Browse use cases
AI agent platforms are helpful across a wide array of industries. Consider these examples:
Customer support: “U.S. consumers estimate they are transferred at least once during 87% of their customer service interactions." Autonomous customer service agents offer 24/7 support, efficiently handling frequently asked questions (like shipping enquiries or policy details) and providing immediate assistance. They also intelligently route complex issues to human agents, ensuring both speed and quality in customer interactions, ultimately lowering support costs.
Sales optimisation: “Salespeople spend 71% of their time on non-selling tasks (like administrative tasks and manually entering data)." With the help of AI agents, sales organisations can automate the initial stages of the sales process by qualifying and scoring leads based on data-driven insights, allowing sales teams to prioritise high-potential prospects. Furthermore, they can automate follow-up communications and integrate with CRM systems to streamline workflows and improve sales effectiveness.
Sales development representatives (SDR): AI Agent Platforms empower sales development representatives by automating crucial tasks like intelligent lead enrichment, personalised outreach suggestions, automated follow-up sequences, and meeting scheduling. By providing deeper prospect insights and streamlining engagement workflows, these platforms enable SDRs to focus on more strategic interactions, personalise outreach at scale, and ultimately generate a higher volume of qualified leads, significantly boosting their efficiency and contribution to the sales pipeline.
Marketing automation: AI agent platforms revolutionise marketing automation by intelligently managing and executing multi-channel campaigns, dynamically personalising customer journeys based on real-time behaviour and preferences. These agents can automate content delivery across various touchpoints, optimise campaign timing and messaging for maximum engagement, and continuously analyse performance data to automatically adjust strategies for improved lead nurturing, customer retention, and overall marketing ROI. This moves beyond simple rule-based automation to deliver truly adaptive and effective marketing experiences.
eCommerce: AI Agent software technology transforms e-commerce by personalising shopping with tailored recommendations and dynamic content, offering instant customer support, optimising pricing and promotions, enhancing inventory management and forecasting, improving fraud detection, and enabling targeted marketing. This empowers businesses to increase customer satisfaction, streamline operations, maximise revenue, and gain a competitive edge online. In fact, “76% of ecommerce teams with AI credit it with revenue growth."
Healthcare: AI agent software is revolutionising healthcare by automating routine yet vital tasks such as efficient appointment scheduling, continuous patient monitoring via connected devices, and timely medication reminders, ultimately leading to improved patient care outcomes and a significant reduction in the administrative workload for healthcare professionals.
Choosing the ideal AI Agent Platform is a critical decision that hinges on your organisation's specific needs and technical capabilities. Let's explore some prominent players in the market. We built our top picks list around trusted G2 ratings, ensuring every solution scored at least 4 out of 5 stars. G2's star ratings are powered by user reviews covering usability, customer satisfaction, and market presence:
As a leading AI Agent Platform, Salesforce Agentforce empowers businesses to build and deploy intelligent agents
seamlessly within the Salesforce ecosystem. Leveraging the power of Einstein AI, Agentforce excels at augmenting customer service, sales processes, and various internal workflows. Its intuitive interface caters to both technical and non-technical users, offering robust no-code/low-code tools for agent creation and management. Agentforce deeply integrates with Salesforce's CRM and other cloud services, providing a unified data foundation for AI-driven automation. With a strong emphasis on security and scalability, it's an excellent choice for organisations looking to enhance their Salesforce investment with intelligent automation.
Microsoft Copilot offers AI-powered assistance integrated across various Microsoft 365 applications. Whilst not solely an AI Agent Platform, Copilot provides intelligent automation for tasks like content creation, data analysis, and meeting summarisation. Its strength lies in its ubiquitous integration within the Microsoft ecosystem, offering convenient AI-powered productivity enhancements for many users.
ServiceNow provides a platform with AI-powered automation capabilities within its Now Assist feature set. Focussed on improving service management and operational efficiency, ServiceNow’s AI features can automate tasks, predict issues, and enhance agent productivity within the ServiceNow environment. Its AI functionalities are tightly integrated with its ITSM , CSM, and other workflow solutions.
Relevance AI offers a platform that builds AI agents for various business applications, emphasising ease of use and flexibility. It provides tools for connecting to diverse data sources and building custom AI-powered workflows.
When selecting AI agent software, consider these crucial factors to ensure the chosen solution aligns with your organisational requirements:
Technical expertise: Determine if a no-code, low-code, or code-based platform best suits your team's technical skills.
Specific use cases: Prioritise platforms that offer capabilities that directly address your desired automation tasks and business needs.
Integration needs: Confirm seamless integration with your existing systems, applications, and data repositories.
Security requirements: Thoroughly evaluate the platform's security protocols and compliance certifications to meet your data privacy standards.
Scalability: Choose a platform that can adapt and expand alongside your evolving automation requirements and business growth.
Budget: Analyse the pricing models of different platforms to identify a solution that aligns with your financial constraints.
AI agent platforms are revolutionising the way we do business by by automating a broad range of tasks and workflows and freeing human workers to pursue more creative and strategic endeavours. As AI technology progresses, AI agent software will only become more adaptable and more effective at work. Ready to explore the transformative potential of AI agent platforms? Explore Salesforce Trailhead to see how to easily deploy AI Agents and AI-powered automation.
What do AI agent platforms do?
AI agent platforms are special software systems that help create, run, and manage AI agents . They provide the tools and environment for these smart programmes to operate independently and achieve specific goals. These platforms handle everything from letting you build an agent to monitoring its performance. They help businesses use AI agents to automate complex tasks and make smarter decisions across different operations.
What are key components of AI agent platforms?
AI agent platforms typically have several important parts. They include tools for building the agents, often with pre-built modules or coding options. A key component is the orchestration engine , which helps agents plan their actions and connect with other systems. They also have components for managing data, allowing agents to learn and make decisions. Finally, monitoring tools are essential for tracking the agents’ performance and making sure they work correctly.
What are the benefits of AI agent platforms?
AI agent platforms offer many benefits for businesses. They make it easier to develop and deploy powerful AI agents quickly, even for those without deep coding knowledge. These platforms help automate complex business processes, leading to significant time and cost savings. They also provide better control and oversight of AI agents, ensuring they operate safely and effectively. Ultimately, they help businesses innovate faster and achieve new levels of efficiency.
What are the differences between cloud-based and on-premise AI agent platforms?
Cloud-based AI agent platforms are hosted and managed by a third party on the internet. This means businesses don't need to buy or maintain their own hardware, making them easier and quicker to start using. On-premise AI agent platforms are installed and run directly on a company's own servers and infrastructure. This offers more control over data and security, but requires the company to manage all the hardware and software themselves. The choice depends on a business's specific needs for control, cost, and ease of use.

Share the story
Try Agentforce
Are you feeling the pressure to do more with less? You're not alone. From automotive to aviation, companies in every industry are looking for innovative ways to streamline operations and improve customer experiences. AI agent platforms offer a promising solution, enabling businesses to develop, deploy, and manage autonomous AI agents to execute tasks and achieve specific goals with minimal human intervention.

Seamless Salesforce Integration: Agent Builder is deeply integrated with the Salesforce ecosystem, allowing you to easily connect agents to your existing Salesforce data, business logic (Flows, Apex), APIs (MuleSoft), and pre-built Prompt Templates.
Built-in Testing and Monitoring: Agent Builder includes tools like Plan Tracer, enabling you to preview and test how your agents will respond to queries, ensuring optimal performance and facilitating iterative development.
Find out more
AI knowledge bases are integral components of modern data and AI platforms, serving as comprehensive repositories that store, organise, and manage a wide array of data and insights. These repositories are designed to support AI-driven decision-making and automation, ensuring that AI agents and systems have access to the most relevant and up-to-date information.
Key features include:
Real-time insights : AI Knowledge Bases provide real-time access to data, enabling AI agents to make immediate and informed decisions. This is crucial for dynamic environments where data is constantly changing.
Data integration : These knowledge bases seamlessly integrate with various data sources, including structured and unstructured data, ensuring a holistic view of the information. This integration supports comprehensive data analysis and more accurate AI-driven insights.
Data governance : Robust data governance features ensure that the information in the knowledge base is secure, compliant, and of high quality. This is essential for maintaining trust and reliability in AI-driven processes.
AI agents can seamlessly access and utilise the knowledge stored in these bases to perform tasks. By providing agents with a robust and dynamic knowledge base, organisations can ensure that their AI systems make data-driven decisions, leading to fewer errors and biases and ultimately improving outcomes.
Implementing an AI agent platform can bring a wealth of benefits to your organisation, fundamentally transforming the way you operate, interact with customers, and ultimately achieve business objectives. This software moves beyond simple automation, offering intelligent assistance and proactive capabilities that can drive significant improvements across various facets of the enterprise.
Increased efficiency and productivity: By automating repetitive and time-consuming tasks, AI agents free up your employees to focus on more strategic and creative work. This leads to increased efficiency and productivity across the board. Automating tasks, saving time and money, and boosting productivity are key advantages.
Reduced costs: AI agent platforms can improve resource utilisation by optimising processes and reducing waste.
Improved customer experience: AI agents are at your service around the clock, providing immediate responses to customer queries, which can significantly boost satisfaction and loyalty. Moreover, they can customise interactions to cater to each customer's distinct needs and preferences. The convenience of 24/7 customer support is invaluable.
Enhanced decision-making: AI agent software solutions have the ability to analyse large volumes of data, offering data-driven insights and recommendations that can lead to more informed decisions.
Scalability and flexibility: AI agent platforms can easily handle increasing workloads, ensuring that your automation efforts can scale as your business grows. They are also adaptable to changing business needs, allowing you to quickly adjust your automation strategies to meet new challenges.
Browse use cases
AI agent platforms are helpful across a wide array of industries. Consider these examples:
Customer support: “U.S. consumers estimate they are transferred at least once during 87% of their customer service interactions." Autonomous customer service agents offer 24/7 support, efficiently handling frequently asked questions (like shipping enquiries or policy details) and providing immediate assistance. They also intelligently route complex issues to human agents, ensuring both speed and quality in customer interactions, ultimately lowering support costs.
Sales optimisation: “Salespeople spend 71% of their time on non-selling tasks (like administrative tasks and manually entering data)." With the help of AI agents, sales organisations can automate the initial stages of the sales process by qualifying and scoring leads based on data-driven insights, allowing sales teams to prioritise high-potential prospects. Furthermore, they can automate follow-up communications and integrate with CRM systems to streamline workflows and improve sales effectiveness.
Sales development representatives (SDR): AI Agent Platforms empower sales development representatives by automating crucial tasks like intelligent lead enrichment, personalised outreach suggestions, automated follow-up sequences, and meeting scheduling. By providing deeper prospect insights and streamlining engagement workflows, these platforms enable SDRs to focus on more strategic interactions, personalise outreach at scale, and ultimately generate a higher volume of qualified leads, significantly boosting their efficiency and contribution to the sales pipeline.
Marketing automation: AI agent platforms revolutionise marketing automation by intelligently managing and executing multi-channel campaigns, dynamically personalising customer journeys based on real-time behaviour and preferences. These agents can automate content delivery across various touchpoints, optimise campaign timing and messaging for maximum engagement, and continuously analyse performance data to automatically adjust strategies for improved lead nurturing, customer retention, and overall marketing ROI. This moves beyond simple rule-based automation to deliver truly adaptive and effective marketing experiences.
eCommerce: AI Agent software technology transforms e-commerce by personalising shopping with tailored recommendations and dynamic content, offering instant customer support, optimising pricing and promotions, enhancing inventory management and forecasting, improving fraud detection, and enabling targeted marketing. This empowers businesses to increase customer satisfaction, streamline operations, maximise revenue, and gain a competitive edge online. In fact, “76% of ecommerce teams with AI credit it with revenue growth."
Healthcare: AI agent software is revolutionising healthcare by automating routine yet vital tasks such as efficient appointment scheduling, continuous patient monitoring via connected devices, and timely medication reminders, ultimately leading to improved patient care outcomes and a significant reduction in the administrative workload for healthcare professionals.
Choosing the ideal AI Agent Platform is a critical decision that hinges on your organisation's specific needs and technical capabilities. Let's explore some prominent players in the market. We built our top picks list around trusted G2 ratings, ensuring every solution scored at least 4 out of 5 stars. G2's star ratings are powered by user reviews covering usability, customer satisfaction, and market presence:
As a leading AI Agent Platform, Salesforce Agentforce empowers businesses to build and deploy intelligent agents
seamlessly within the Salesforce ecosystem. Leveraging the power of Einstein AI, Agentforce excels at augmenting customer service, sales processes, and various internal workflows. Its intuitive interface caters to both technical and non-technical users, offering robust no-code/low-code tools for agent creation and management. Agentforce deeply integrates with Salesforce's CRM and other cloud services, providing a unified data foundation for AI-driven automation. With a strong emphasis on security and scalability, it's an excellent choice for organisations looking to enhance their Salesforce investment with intelligent automation.
Microsoft Copilot offers AI-powered assistance integrated across various Microsoft 365 applications. Whilst not solely an AI Agent Platform, Copilot provides intelligent automation for tasks like content creation, data analysis, and meeting summarisation. Its strength lies in its ubiquitous integration within the Microsoft ecosystem, offering convenient AI-powered productivity enhancements for many users.
ServiceNow provides a platform with AI-powered automation capabilities within its Now Assist feature set. Focussed on improving service management and operational efficiency, ServiceNow’s AI features can automate tasks, predict issues, and enhance agent productivity within the ServiceNow environment. Its AI functionalities are tightly integrated with its ITSM , CSM, and other workflow solutions.
Relevance AI offers a platform that builds AI agents for various business applications, emphasising ease of use and flexibility. It provides tools for connecting to diverse data sources and building custom AI-powered workflows.
When selecting AI agent software, consider these crucial factors to ensure the chosen solution aligns with your organisational requirements:
Technical expertise: Determine if a no-code, low-code, or code-based platform best suits your team's technical skills.
Specific use cases: Prioritise platforms that offer capabilities that directly address your desired automation tasks and business needs.
Integration needs: Confirm seamless integration with your existing systems, applications, and data repositories.
Security requirements: Thoroughly evaluate the platform's security protocols and compliance certifications to meet your data privacy standards.
Scalability: Choose a platform that can adapt and expand alongside your evolving automation requirements and business growth.
Budget: Analyse the pricing models of different platforms to identify a solution that aligns with your financial constraints.
AI agent platforms are revolutionising the way we do business by by automating a broad range of tasks and workflows and freeing human workers to pursue more creative and strategic endeavours. As AI technology progresses, AI agent software will only become more adaptable and more effective at work. Ready to explore the transformative potential of AI agent platforms? Explore Salesforce Trailhead to see how to easily deploy AI Agents and AI-powered automation.
What do AI agent platforms do?
AI agent platforms are special software systems that help create, run, and manage AI agents . They provide the tools and environment for these smart programmes to operate independently and achieve specific goals. These platforms handle everything from letting you build an agent to monitoring its performance. They help businesses use AI agents to automate complex tasks and make smarter decisions across different operations.
What are key components of AI agent platforms?
AI agent platforms typically have several important parts. They include tools for building the agents, often with pre-built modules or coding options. A key component is the orchestration engine , which helps agents plan their actions and connect with other systems. They also have components for managing data, allowing agents to learn and make decisions. Finally, monitoring tools are essential for tracking the agents’ performance and making sure they work correctly.
What are the benefits of AI agent platforms?
AI agent platforms offer many benefits for businesses. They make it easier to develop and deploy powerful AI agents quickly, even for those without deep coding knowledge. These platforms help automate complex business processes, leading to significant time and cost savings. They also provide better control and oversight of AI agents, ensuring they operate safely and effectively. Ultimately, they help businesses innovate faster and achieve new levels of efficiency.
What are the differences between cloud-based and on-premise AI agent platforms?
Cloud-based AI agent platforms are hosted and managed by a third party on the internet. This means businesses don't need to buy or maintain their own hardware, making them easier and quicker to start using. On-premise AI agent platforms are installed and run directly on a company's own servers and infrastructure. This offers more control over data and security, but requires the company to manage all the hardware and software themselves. The choice depends on a business's specific needs for control, cost, and ease of use.

Share the story
Try Agentforce
Are you feeling the pressure to do more with less? You're not alone. From automotive to aviation, companies in every industry are looking for innovative ways to streamline operations and improve customer experiences. AI agent platforms offer a promising solution, enabling businesses to develop, deploy, and manage autonomous AI agents to execute tasks and achieve specific goals with minimal human intervention.
AI agent platforms help organisations save time and money, boost productivity, and enhance decision-making across key business functions. This guide will explore the essential components, benefits, real-world use cases, and future trends of this transformative technology.
At its core, an AI agent is a type of artificial intelligence system that can understand and respond to customer inquiries without human intervention. They are created through an agent builder , such as Agentforce and use machine learning and natural language processing (NLP) to handle a variety of tasks. Agents thrive on high-quality data, pulling in and synthesising information from disparate sources to inform their decision-making and actions.
AI agent platforms are characterised by these essential capabilities:
Autonomy: Agents operate independently, making decisions and taking action without continuous human intervention.
Learning: Agents continuously improve their performance by adapting and refining strategies based on experience, leading to enhanced outcomes over time.
Adaptability: Agents readily adjust to evolving environments and diverse tasks, maintaining effectiveness even when faced with novel challenges.
Goal-oriented: Agents are designed to achieve defined objectives, whether it's optimising workflows, enhancing customer experiences, or managing complex operations.
Security: Robust measures are implemented to ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of agents, their data, and their operations.
Interaction & communication: Agents can effectively interact and communicate with other agents, diverse systems, and potentially humans through various methods, enabling seamless collaboration and information exchange.
An AI agent platform is more than just a single piece of software; it's a complete AI, data, and security ecosystem that provides all the necessary tools and infrastructure for developing and managing AI agents. The essential components include:
Component
Function
Agent builder
Facilitates the creation of AI agents
Reasoning engine
Analyses data, identifies patterns, and makes decisions
Action
Executes tasks and interacts with users or other systems
Learning
Improves performance over time through feedback and new data
Orchestration
Coordinates multiple agents to work together on complex workflows
Governance
Applies security, compliance, and operational controls
Agent Builder offers an accessible, AI-driven, low-code tool for building and customising AI agents to meet any business need. Users can describe an agent's purpose using natural language, leverage pre-built topics to define the agent's scope of work, and seamlessly integrate with knowledge bases and existing business logic.
Intuitive builder environment: Agent Builder offers a user-friendly, AI-powered, low-code interface for visually designing and configuring your AI agents. This “digital workshop” provides a streamlined experience for users of all technical levels.
Low-code focus with pro-code extensibility: Agent Builder emphasises a low-code approach, allowing you to build agents through configuration and natural language descriptions. However, it also provides the flexibility to extend agent capabilities using pro-code tools like Apex Classes and integrations with other Salesforce development resources.
AI-Powered Assistance: Agent Builder can assist in suggesting topics and actions and even generate initial agent configurations based on your descriptions and Salesforce metadata.
Seamless Salesforce Integration: Agent Builder is deeply integrated with the Salesforce ecosystem, allowing you to easily connect agents to your existing Salesforce data, business logic (Flows, Apex), APIs (MuleSoft), and pre-built Prompt Templates.
Built-in Testing and Monitoring: Agent Builder includes tools like Plan Tracer, enabling you to preview and test how your agents will respond to queries, ensuring optimal performance and facilitating iterative development.
